title: aster lateriflorus var. hirsuticaulis
intro: Aster lateriflorus var. hirsuticaulis, also known as hairy leafy aster, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family, characterized by its hairy stems and leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of hairy leafy aster include reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting relaxation, making it a popular herbal remedy for stress and anxiety. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and can also be applied topically as a poultice or salve to treat skin conditions and wounds.
